response.content: {"suggestions": ["Proxy-based outbound data control: Route all outbound network traffic from the UbuntuComputer through the existing Squid proxy with strict ACLs and user authentication. Configure Squid to block direct access to external hosts for sensitive operations, and restrict uploads to internal or approved destinations only. This limits exfiltration pathways and provides centralized visibility and control over data leaving the host.", "Network segmentation and enforced egress restrictions: Strengthen firewall rules (Fortigate) to deny direct internet egress from the UbuntuComputer subnet. Require that outbound traffic must traverse the proxy gateway and internal security devices. This limits attacker ability to reach external C2 endpoints and compresses exfil paths to monitored channels.", "Endpoint confinement for critical processes: Implement AppArmor (or SELinux) profiles to constrain the UbuntuComputer\u2019s critical processes (e.g., Splunkd) to only the minimum required filesystem paths and network destinations. This reduces the attacker\u2019s ability to access staging directories or initiate privileged exfiltration actions even while the host remains compromised.", "Enhanced monitoring and real-time alerting for exfiltration activity: Enable comprehensive logging and alerting around data staging, tar/zip creation, and HTTP file upload attempts. Ensure Squid, Fortigate, and host OS logs are forwarded to a SIEM or centralized monitoring tool, with real-time alerts for staging directory creation, copies to staging, archive creation, and attempts to exfil via HTTP."], "chosen_mitigation": "Proxy-based outbound data control: Route all outbound network traffic from the UbuntuComputer through the existing Squid proxy with strict ACLs and user authentication. Configure Squid to block direct access to external hosts for sensitive operations, and restrict uploads to internal or approved destinations only. This limits exfiltration pathways and provides centralized visibility and control over data leaving the host.", "no_mitigation_left": false, "current_mitigations_counter": 1, "reached_mitigation_limit": false, "mitigation_start_timestamp": "2025-12-30T10:39:52.446573"}
